subject: Mozi Minute: When It’s Easy, Go Harder
body:
Words I like: Most entrepreneurs celebrate when business gets easy, but the smartest ones recognize that's exactly when they should accelerate.

Mozi Minute: When It's Easy, Go Harder

Business is like a casino. If you keep playing with the same strategy and never change the odds, the house eventually wins.

The market is the house.

Competition is the house.

Bad luck is the house.

They all have infinite time and resources.

You don't.

But here's what most entrepreneurs miss: when you get a good hand, you don't celebrate and coast. You double down.

Most business owners do the opposite. Revenue is flowing, team is running smooth, customers are happy.

So they ease up. Take longer vacations. Focus on "work-life balance."

Big mistake.

Easy times are when you build the moat that protects you during hard times.

When business gets easy, that's your signal to go harder.

Here's why:

Easy means you have excess capacity. Use it to build systems, hire better people, expand into new markets. Don't waste it on comfort.

Easy means you're ahead of competitors. While they're struggling with the problems you've solved, you can pull further ahead. Gaps close fast in business.

Easy means cash flow is strong. This is when you invest in the future, not when you increase personal expenses. Reinvest while you can afford to fail.

Easy is temporary. Markets shift. Competitors catch up. Economic conditions change. The entrepreneurs who survive understand that easy times fund the hard times.

Think of every easy period as borrowed time.

The casino will eventually get its edge back.

Your job is to change the fundamental odds while you have the advantage.

Real examples:

Amazon used profitable quarters to fund AWS.

Apple used iPhone success to build their services ecosystem.

They didn't coast when things got easy - they accelerated.

The casino analogy holds:

When you're winning, increase your bets strategically.

When you're losing, preserve capital and wait for better odds.

But never coast just because you're comfortable.

As promised, value in under a minute.

Easy is your opportunity, not just your reward,

Alex
